title: placolobium cambodianum
intro: Placolobium cambodianum is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to Cambodia and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including malaria, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Placolobium cambodianum are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
